question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/main.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: newBlockchainOnlineRouter()] Can an unprivileged external user send a /construction/submit request with a crafted signed transaction that targets a specific node account ID (from getRandomNodeAccountId()), causing all submissions to be routed to a single node and creating a bottleneck that delays block production by 500% or more of the 24-hour average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/main.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: newBlockchainOnlineRouter()] Can an unprivileged external user send a flood of /construction/submit requests with valid but duplicate signed transactions, causing the Hiero SDK client (sdkClient with SetMaxAttempts(1)) to attempt submission of each and exhaust the node's transaction processing capacity, delaying block production by 500% or more of the 24-hour average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/main.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: newBlockchainOnlineRouter()] Can an unprivileged external user send a /construction/payloads request with a metadata map containing a node_account_id pointing to a non-existent or offline node, causing transactionSetNodeAccountId() to set an invalid node and the subsequent submit to hang until timeout, delaying block inclusion by 500% or more of the average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/main.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: newBlockchainOnlineRouter()] Can an unprivileged external user send a /block request with a PartialBlockIdentifier index set to a value just below the genesis block index, causing findBlockByIndex() to return ErrBlockNotFound after a DB query, and by repeating this at high frequency, saturate the DB error handling path and delay legitimate block queries by 500% or more of the average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/main.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: newBlockchainOnlineRouter()] Can an unprivileged external user send a /block request with a PartialBlockIdentifier hash containing only whitespace or special characters, causing the selectByHashWithIndex query with collate 'C' to perform unexpected string comparison behavior and hold a DB connection longer than expected, delaying block time by 500% or more of the 24-hour average?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/main.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ReadTimeout of the http.Server in main() by sending requests with a very slow body delivery rate that stays just within the ReadTimeout threshold, holding server goroutines busy and preventing block-related requests from being processed, causing block delays exceeding 500% of the 24-hour average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/main.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: newBlockchainOnlineRouter()] Can an unprivileged external user send a /block request with a PartialBlockIdentifier containing both index and hash fields where the hash is a valid hex string of exactly 96 characters (matching a real block hash format), causing FindByIdentifier() to execute both findBlockByHash and index validation sequentially and delay block processing by 500% or more of the average block time under concurrent load?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/main.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: newBlockchainOnlineRouter()] Can an unprivileged external user send a /account/balance request with a BlockIdentifier pointing to a very old block, causing the accountRepo to execute a historical balance query that scans a large range of the record_file table and holds a DB connection long enough to delay current block processing by 500% or more of the 24-hour average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/main.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: newBlockchainOnlineRouter()] Can an unprivileged external user send a /network/status request that triggers RetrieveLatest() while simultaneously flooding /block requests, causing the blockRepository's DB connection pool (Pool.MaxOpenConnections) to be exhausted and delaying the latest block's consensus_end timestamp from being updated, effectively freezing block time by 500% or more of the average?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/main.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: newBlockchainOnlineRouter()] Can an unprivileged external user send a /construction/combine request with a very large number of signatures (each requiring ed25519.Verify() computation), causing the CPU to be saturated with signature verification and delaying block-related request processing by 500% or more of the 24-hour average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/main.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: newBlockchainOnlineRouter()] Can an unprivileged external user send a /construction/parse request with a crafted hex-encoded transaction containing a very large memo field, causing TransactionGetTransactionMemo() to allocate excessive memory and delay block-related request processing by 500% or more of the 24-hour average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/main.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: newBlockchainOnlineRouter()] Can an unprivileged external user send a /construction/hash request with a hex-encoded transaction string of maximum possible length, causing hex.DecodeString() and TransactionFromBytes() to consume excessive memory and CPU, delaying block processing by 500% or more of the 24-hour average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/main.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: newBlockchainOnlineRouter()] Can an unprivileged external user send a /construction/preprocess request with a very large operations array, causing getOperationSlice() to iterate over all operations and perform NewAccountIdFromString() for each, consuming excessive CPU and delaying block-related processing by 500% or more of the 24-hour average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/main.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: newBlockchainOnlineRouter()] Can an unprivileged external user send a /block request with a PartialBlockIdentifier index set to a very large positive integer (e.g., int64 max - 1), causing the selectRecordBlockByIndex query to perform an index seek on a non-existent row and hold a DB connection, and by repeating this at high frequency, delay legitimate block queries by 500% or more of the average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/main.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: newBlockchainOnlineRouter()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the coalesce subquery in selectRecordBlockByIndex (which selects consensus_start-1 from the next record_file) by requesting a block at the tip of the chain, causing the subquery to return NULL and fall back to consensus_end, and by flooding such requests, delay block time reporting by 500% or more of the 24-hour average?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/main.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: newBlockchainOnlineRouter()] Can an unprivileged external user send a /block request with a PartialBlockIdentifier hash set to a string that matches the genesis block hash, causing ToBlock() to execute the genesis edge case (parentHash = rb.Hash, parentIndex = rb.Index) and by flooding such requests, delay block processing by 500% or more of the 24-hour average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/main.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the CorsMiddleware (server.CorsMiddleware) in main() by sending preflight OPTIONS requests at high frequency, causing the CORS handler to process each request and delay actual block-related requests by 500% or more of the 24-hour average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/main.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: newBlockchainOnlineRouter()] Can an unprivileged external user send a /block request with a PartialBlockIdentifier index set to 0 (genesis index), causing initGenesisRecordFile() to be called and the selectGenesis query to join account_balance with record_file, and by flooding such requests before genesis is cached, delay block initialization by 500% or more of the average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/main.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: newBlockchainOnlineRouter()] Can an unprivileged external user send a /block request with a PartialBlockIdentifier hash containing a valid hex string that does not match any block, causing findBlockByHash() to execute selectByHashWithIndex with a collate 'C' comparison that scans the entire record_file table, and by repeating this, delay block processing by 500% or more of the 24-hour average block time?
